close_reason: Positive observation: UFCS for SigningKey::as_ref is correct
description:
lib.rs:237 uses fully-qualified syntax: <rsa::pss::SigningKey<_> as AsRef<rsa::RsaPrivateKey>>::as_ref(sk). This LOOKS like overengineered Rust; the natural form is sk.as_ref().

It is REQUIRED: rsa::pss::SigningKey implements both AsRef<rsa::RsaPrivateKey> (the one we want) AND the blanket impl<T: ?Sized> AsRef<T> for &T (the standard library's). Method-call syntax sk.as_ref() resolves to whichever the type-checker can infer; in this context it picks the wrong one and the code does not compile, OR worse, infers Self ↔ Self.

The comment at lib.rs:232-235 documents this. Anyone trying to 'simplify' it will hit the trait-resolution error and learn. DEFEND.
title: DEFEND: UFCS for SigningKey::as_ref disambiguation in public_key_der
